item.title: Widespread damage to the road surface, 11th March
item.description:
Very dangerous for cyclists passing through the area, there is no option to cycle on the pavement, due to bus pedestrian area and tree roots. Fix needs to be escalated as this is urgent.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Malvern Road, Alstone, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: This pothole is becoming ridiculous, 11th March
item.description:
Please fix it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Badgeworth Road, Badgeworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole - customers in Tesco have damaged their cars with a suspension arm snapping, 11th March
item.description:
The pothole is located where the pavement meets the road by the exit out of the Tescp carpark.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Suggestion - Confusing lane markings and missing exit on roundabout signage, 11th March
item.description:
At Arle Court Roundabout the lane markings and signage are inconsistent and regularly cause confusion. On the approach from the A40 via Hatherley Lane, both lanes are marked as straight ahead. However, after passing the second exit the right lane becomes marked “right turn only.” This means drivers using the right lane to go straight suddenly find themselves in a lane designated only for turning right. In addition, the main roundabout sign does not show the second exit into the retail park / B&Q Cheltenham car park, even though traffic frequently exits here. A temporary yellow sign currently does show this exit, suggesting the permanent signage may be incomplete. Clearer signage and consistent lane markings would likely reduce hesitation and last-minute lane changes at this busy junction.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hatherley Lane, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Road marking white lines or studs

item.title: TMC - Defects - Covers, Gratings & Frames problem, 11th March
item.description:
28 day 4 way traffic lights lift and reset sunken rocking manhole cover in cway STATS. Existing traffic signals will require suspension. (Broken Chamber Cover/Frame)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Telephone Box, 11th March
item.description:
Smashed glass in old phone box. Located on the pedestrian walkway on the Promenade, Cheltenham

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Promenade, Montpellier, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface
